coordinate_system: Atlas cell coordinates [x,y] from the top-left of each source image. Ground_rocks.png is treated as a 64x64 atlas for the current AI-assisted workflow.

ground_rocks.usage: Terrain candidate source only. Most cells remain excluded because they contain cliff faces, transparent holes, edge fragments, or corner-only shapes.

objects.usage: Not used in this ground catalog. Props should be cataloged separately.

exclusion_rules: Do not use cells where a blue/dark vertical cliff face is the main visible area.
Do not use cells with large white or transparent background holes.
Do not use obvious corner-only shapes as base ground.
Do not use broken edge fragments as base ground.
Do not use Objects.png for base floor.

floor_detail_small.reason: Small crack and pebble detail; suitable as an overlay only.
Small crack detail; suitable as an overlay only.
Small crack and pebble detail; suitable as an overlay only.
Small crack detail; suitable as an overlay only.
Sparse small rock detail; suitable as an overlay only.
Sparse small rock and shard detail; suitable as an overlay only.

maybe_ground.reason: Mostly rough rubble, but may be a broken transition rather than a safe walkable floor.
Mostly flat dark-green mass, but its material/role is unclear.
Mostly rough ground-looking area, but it includes edge-like material and needs visual validation.

maybe_border.reason: Border-like top surface, but contains transparent/background area.
Border-like top surface, but contains transparent/background area.
Possible border/corner transition; not safe enough for automatic use.
Possible border cap, but includes an internal hole.
Border-like top surface, but includes broken edge fragments.
Border-like top surface, but includes broken edge fragments.
Border-like top surface, but includes broken edge fragments.
